python.code:
import boto3

# AWS Auto Scaling example demonstrating Rapid Elasticity
client = boto3.client('autoscaling')

# Create Auto Scaling Group
response = client.create_auto_scaling_group(
    AutoScalingGroupName='my-asg',
    LaunchConfigurationName='my-launch-config',
    MinSize=2,          # Minimum instances (scale IN limit)
    MaxSize=10,         # Maximum instances (scale OUT limit)
    DesiredCapacity=2,  # Starting number
    AvailabilityZones=['us-west-2a', 'us-west-2b'],
    Tags=[
        {'Key': 'Environment', 'Value': 'Production'}
    ]
)

# Create scaling policy (Measured Service + Elasticity)
policy = client.put_scaling_policy(
    AutoScalingGroupName='my-asg',
    PolicyName='scale-out-policy',
    PolicyType='TargetTrackingScaling',
    TargetTrackingConfiguration={
        'PredefinedMetricSpecification': {
            'PredefinedMetricType': 'ASGAverageCPUUtilization'
        },
        'TargetValue': 70.0  # Scale when CPU > 70%
    }
)

print('Auto Scaling Group created with elasticity!')

yaml.code:
# Kubernetes Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (Rapid Elasticity)
apiVersion: autoscaling/v2
kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler
metadata:
  name: my-app-hpa
spec:
  scaleTargetRef:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: my-app
  minReplicas: 2      # Minimum pods
  maxReplicas: 10     # Maximum pods (elasticity)
  metrics:
  - type: Resource
    resource:
      name: cpu
      target:
        type: Utilization
        averageUtilization: 70  # Scale at 70% CPU
  - type: Resource
    resource:
      name: memory
      target:
        type: Utilization
        averageUtilization: 80
---
# Demonstrates: Resource Pooling + Rapid Elasticity + Measured Service

bash.code:
#!/bin/bash
# Cloud CLI examples demonstrating characteristics

# ON-DEMAND SELF-SERVICE: Create VM instantly
aws ec2 run-instances \
    --image-id ami-0c55b159cbfafe1f0 \
    --instance-type t2.micro \
    --count 1

# MEASURED SERVICE: Check current usage
aws ce get-cost-and-usage \
    --time-period Start=2024-01-01,End=2024-01-31 \
    --granularity MONTHLY \
    --metrics "BlendedCost" "UsageQuantity"

# RAPID ELASTICITY: Update auto-scaling limits
aws autoscaling update-auto-scaling-group \
    --auto-scaling-group-name my-asg \
    --min-size 2 \
    --max-size 20  # Increase max for more elasticity

# BROAD NETWORK ACCESS: Access from anywhere
curl https://my-app.cloud-provider.com/api/health

echo "Cloud characteristics in action!"
